title: Commission seeks feedback on potential simplification of the European Maritime, Fisheries and Aquaculture Fund
content: The European Commission is seeking feedback in view of a possible targeted amendment of the European Maritime, Fisheries and Aquaculture Fund (EMFAF). The objective is to make the fund simpler, more flexible and easier to implement while implementing also in EU law the WTO Agreement
snippet: The European Commission is seeking feedback in view of a possible targeted amendment of the European Maritime, Fisheries and Aquaculture Fund (EMFAF). The objective is to make the fund simpler, more

title: Call for applications: candidate energy infrastructure Projects of Common and Mutual Interest - hydrogen, electrolyser and CO2 category
content: Today, the Commission has opened a call for applications under the TEN-E Regulation for energy infrastructure projects in the hydrogen, electrolyser and CO₂ categories seeking Project of Common Interest (PCI) or Project of Mutual Interest (PMI) status.

title: Commission reaches preliminary position that Amazon's and Microsoft's market leading cloud services should be designated under the DMA
content: Commission reaches preliminary position that Amazon's and Microsoft's market leading cloud services should be designated under the DMA

title: Report highlights EU’s continued efforts to improve effective control of exports of sensitive items
content: A European Commission report evaluating export patterns of dual-use items from EU Member States shows that the EU continues to improve enforcement and implementation of its dual-use export controls framework while providing transparency on exports of dual-use goods in 2024.
snippet: A European Commission report evaluating export patterns of dual-use items from EU Member States shows that the EU continues to improve enforcement and implementation of its dual-use export controls

title: Local governance systems and entrepreneurial learning take centre stage at the 2026 EAfA High-Level Event in Rome
content: Held on 4 and 5 June 2026 in Rome, the European Commission and the Metropolitan City of Rome Capital hosted the 2026 High-Level Event, bringing together around 200 participants, including EAfA members from 12 countries.
snippet: Held on 4 and 5 June 2026 in Rome, the European Commission and the Metropolitan City of Rome Capital hosted the 2026 High-Level Event, bringing together around 200 participants, including EAfA members

title: Simplifying EU rules to support competitiveness
content: By simplifying EU rules on energy products and taxation, the latest omnibus proposals will help support both businesses and people.
snippet: By simplifying EU rules on energy products and taxation, the latest omnibus proposals will help support both businesses and people.

title: Study examines how blue bioeconomy could diversify Northwest fisheries
content: A study by Bord Iascaigh Mhara (BIM) Ireland’s Seafood Development Agency, and Donegal County Council (DCC), examines how the blue bioeconomy could help diversify the fisheries sector in Northwest Ireland, particularly the pelagic fishing industry.
snippet: A study by Bord Iascaigh Mhara (BIM) Ireland’s Seafood Development Agency, and Donegal County Council (DCC), examines how the blue bioeconomy could help diversify the fisheries sector in Northwest

title: Joint call on current and prospective donors to contribute to the Ukraine Energy Support Fund
content: Over the past four years, G7+ countries have channelled €1.95 billion through the Ukraine Energy Support Fund, managed by the Energy Community Secretariat, making a substantial contribution to Ukraine’s energy resilience. All resources available under the Fund have been fully allocated in line with

title: President von der Leyen in Gdańsk for the Ukraine Recovery Conference
content:
Tomorrow, President Ursula von der Leyen will participate in the Ukraine Recovery Conference, in Gdańsk, Poland.
Co-hosted by Poland and Ukraine, the conference scheduled for 25 and 26 June marks the fifth edition of this annual high-level event dedicated to mobilising international support for...

title: Commission simplifies and makes energy and tyre labelling rules more accessible to European consumers
content: European consumers, suppliers and retailers of home appliances, electronic devices and tyres are set to benefit from simplified rules on labelling proposed by the European Commission today.
snippet: European consumers, suppliers and retailers of home appliances, electronic devices and tyres are set to benefit from simplified rules on labelling proposed by the European Commission today.

title: Shaping the European Housing Alliance: turning principles into action
content: The European Housing Alliance held its inaugural meeting in Brussels on 19 June, marking a critical step in implementing one of the priorities of the European Affordable Housing Plan adopted by the European Commission in December 2025.
snippet: The European Housing Alliance held its inaugural meeting in Brussels on 19 June, marking a critical step in implementing one of the priorities of the European Affordable Housing Plan adopted by the

title: Apply before 16 August: Do you have direct experience with social, affordable and sustainable housing financing?
content: The Commission is looking for stakeholders with direct experience with financing of social, affordable and sustainable housing. A new working group will bring expertise together and share best practices.
snippet: The Commission is looking for stakeholders with direct experience with financing of social, affordable and sustainable housing. A new working group will bring expertise together and share best

title: Two-thirds of gas appliances fail EU checks
content: EU-funded testing of a selection of gas appliances like barbecues, gas stoves and camping cookers found that 73 % failed to meet EU requirements for performance and product documentation.
snippet: EU-funded testing of a selection of gas appliances like barbecues, gas stoves and camping cookers found that 73 % failed to meet EU requirements for performance and product documentation.

title: The European Commission welcomes NEAFC measures on mackerel and deplores Russia’s further quota increase in 2026
content: The mackerel stock in the North-East Atlantic is currently in a critical conservation status after years of overfishing, particularly by the Russian Federation. In 2026, Russia unilaterally raised its quota to 22.5% of the Total Allowable Catch (TAC) - equivalent to 67,548 tonnes
snippet: The mackerel stock in the North-East Atlantic is currently in a critical conservation status after years of overfishing, particularly by the Russian Federation. In 2026, Russia unilaterally raised its

title: Bonn climate conference keeps momentum towards COP31 with technical negotiations advancing and new action agenda announcements
content: The Bonn UN Climate Change Conference concluded with progress on key technical issues, helping to advance implementation of the Paris Agreement and prepare the ground for COP31 in Türkiye. New initiatives and discussions focused on turning climate commitments into practical action.
snippet: The Bonn UN Climate Change Conference concluded with progress on key technical issues, helping to advance implementation of the Paris Agreement and prepare the ground for COP31 in Türkiye. New

title: Better enforcement of passenger rights: new EU agreement strengthens protection across all modes of transport
content: The Commission welcomes the political agreement reached by the European Parliament and the Council of the EU on improved enforcement of passenger rights across all modes of transport.
snippet: The Commission welcomes the political agreement reached by the European Parliament and the Council of the EU on improved enforcement of passenger rights across all modes of transport.

title: The Commission takes stock of situation on oil markets amid developments in the Middle East
content: In a meeting of the Oil Coordination Group held today, the Commission and EU countries discussed the security of oil supply situation in the EU in light of recent reports on ongoing negotiations in the Middle East.
snippet: In a meeting of the Oil Coordination Group held today, the Commission and EU countries discussed the security of oil supply situation in the EU in light of recent reports on ongoing negotiations in

title: Commission proposes to extend temporary protection of people fleeing Ukraine for an additional year
content: On 26 June, the Commission proposed to extend the temporary protection for people fleeing Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine until 4 March 2028, providing continued legal certainty, stability and predictability to almost 4.4 million people displaced from Ukraine currently living in the EU.
snippet: On 26 June, the Commission proposed to extend the temporary protection for people fleeing Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine until 4 March 2028, providing continued legal certainty, stability

title: Reality Check for the Evaluation of the Firearms Directive
content: This Reality Check meeting marks the first large-scale, direct dialogue between DG HOME and the private firearms sector. Participants included European associations representing firearms and ammunition manufacturers, firearms traders, sport shooters, hunters, and firearms collectors.
snippet: This Reality Check meeting marks the first large-scale, direct dialogue between DG HOME and the private firearms sector. Participants included European associations representing firearms and

title: Projects to Policy Seminar: security research projects feed into policy making
content: The annual Projects to Policy Seminar (PPS) took place on 10 and 11 June in Brussels to gather all the newly begun research projects in the area of civil security for society. This year was a particularly busy edition with over 160 participants drawn from almost 40 new projects.
snippet: The annual Projects to Policy Seminar (PPS) took place on 10 and 11 June in Brussels to gather all the newly begun research projects in the area of civil security for society. This year was a

title: Why Mobility Matters for Europe's Competitiveness
content: From apprentices to entrepreneurs, discover how mobility programmes help build skills, unlock opportunities and strengthen Europe's competitiveness. Join the discussion on 1 July in Brussels or online.
snippet: From apprentices to entrepreneurs, discover how mobility programmes help build skills, unlock opportunities and strengthen Europe's competitiveness. Join the discussion on 1 July in Brussels or

title: Commissioner Síkela concludes Global Gateway investment mission to Brazil
content: Commissioner for International Partnerships Jozef Síkela’s mission to Brazil was about strengthening the EU-Brazil partnership through Global Gateway, the EU’s strategy for investing in joint projects in critical raw materials, sustainable transport, digital technology and clean energy.
snippet: Commissioner for International Partnerships Jozef Síkela’s mission to Brazil was about strengthening the EU-Brazil partnership through Global Gateway, the EU’s strategy for investing in joint projects

title: EU GOVSATCOM Hub Site in Cologne to further strengthen Europe’s Secure Satellite Communications
content: Europe’s secure satellite communications programme took a major step forward last week with the groundbreaking of the GOVSATCOM Site in Cologne, Germany.
snippet: Europe’s secure satellite communications programme took a major step forward last week with the groundbreaking of the GOVSATCOM Site in Cologne, Germany.

title: New study assesses progress in reforming research assessment
content: Expert study ‘Implementing research assessment reform’ showcases progress made in changing research assessment, while identifying remaining gaps.
snippet: Expert study ‘Implementing research assessment reform’ showcases progress made in changing research assessment, while identifying remaining gaps.

title: Environmental protection in upcoming pharmaceutical legislation could be further improved to optimise risk assessments and support implementation
content: Pharmaceuticals entering the natural environment may harm ecosystems and human health. This review assesses current and future legislation designed to mitigate this risk in light of current science.
snippet: Pharmaceuticals entering the natural environment may harm ecosystems and human health. This review assesses current and future legislation designed to mitigate this risk in light of current science.

title: Invasive species ‘break the rules’ of growth in new habitats
content: Alien invasive species are a major driver of global biodiversity loss. A study explores invasive species in Germany, finding these exotic invaders to be drivers of increased plant productivity – but only in their non-native ranges.
snippet: Alien invasive species are a major driver of global biodiversity loss. A study explores invasive species in Germany, finding these exotic invaders to be drivers of increased plant productivity – but

title: Threats to marine ecosystems from plastic waste vary across the world’s oceans
content: A new analysis of marine plastic pollution provides insights for global policy. It identifies the north-eastern Atlantic Ocean as a high-risk area and recommends that ocean cleanup efforts look beyond known marine ‘garbage patches’.
snippet: A new analysis of marine plastic pollution provides insights for global policy. It identifies the north-eastern Atlantic Ocean as a high-risk area and recommends that ocean cleanup efforts look beyond

title: Strategic Dialogue on financing high-speed rail with investors
content: The Commission convened a Strategic Dialogue with investors and rail sector stakeholders to discuss how to scale up financing for high-speed rail infrastructure and rolling stock across the EU.
snippet: The Commission convened a Strategic Dialogue with investors and rail sector stakeholders to discuss how to scale up financing for high-speed rail infrastructure and rolling stock across the EU.

title: Commission reaches preliminary position that Amazon's and Microsoft's market leading cloud services should be designated under the DMA
content: The Commission has informed Amazon and Microsoft of its preliminary view that they should be designated as gatekeepers under the DMA for their cloud computing services, Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Microsoft Azure (Azure) respectively.
snippet: The Commission has informed Amazon and Microsoft of its preliminary view that they should be designated as gatekeepers under the DMA for their cloud computing services, Amazon Web Services (AWS) and

title: Commission disburses the first €3.2 billion instalment to Ukraine under the €90 billion Ukraine Support Loan
content: Today at the Ukraine Recovery Conference, President Ursula von der Leyen announced the disbursement of €3.2 billion to Ukraine as the first instalment of the new macro-financial assistance (MFA).
snippet: Today at the Ukraine Recovery Conference, President Ursula von der Leyen announced the disbursement of €3.2 billion to Ukraine as the first instalment of the new macro-financial assistance (MFA).

title: EU and Egypt strengthen research and innovation partnership following Horizon Europe association
content: 1st EU–Egypt Joint Committee under Horizon Europe & 9th Joint Science and Technology Cooperation Committee Egypt
snippet: 1st EU–Egypt Joint Committee under Horizon Europe & 9th Joint Science and Technology Cooperation Committee Egypt

title: EU steps up support for Ukraine's defence, recovery and reconstruction at the Ukraine Recovery Conference 2026
content: At the Ukraine Recovery Conference 2026, co-hosted by Poland and Ukraine in Gdańsk, President von der Leyen reaffirmed that the European Union will continue to stand firmly by Ukraine, supporting its reforms, strengthening its institutions, and helping build a resilient and prosperous future.

title: New episode – DGT podcast: Languages and Technology
content: Episode 10 of the DGT podcast: Languages and Technology is now available.
snippet: Episode 10 of the DGT podcast: Languages and Technology is now available.

title: Commission imposes anti-dumping duties on imports of BDO from three countries
content: Today, the European Commission published a Regulation imposing definitive anti-dumping duties on imports of 1,4-Butanediol (‘BDO’) originating in the People’s Republic of China, Saudi Arabia and the United States of America.

title: European Commission proposes landmark tax simplification package to streamline compliance and boost competitiveness
content: The European Commission adopted an ambitious tax simplification package that includes two legislative proposals aimed at simplifying EU tax rules, reducing compliance burdens for businesses, and strengthening the competitiveness of the Internal Market.

title: Solving the affordability crisis for young Europeans
content: If rising costs are affecting you as a young person in Europe, then find out what solutions were proposed at this year’s Youth Policy Dialogue in Brussels.
snippet: If rising costs are affecting you as a young person in Europe, then find out what solutions were proposed at this year’s Youth Policy Dialogue in Brussels.

title: Commission strengthens Europol to step up the fight against cross-border crime and terrorism
content: The Commission proposed new measures to strengthen the EU’s response to an evolving criminal landscape. New proposal cover the full chain of EU support to Member States, from prevention, detection and investigation to judicial cooperation and effective prosecution.

title: EQAVET Peer Review on stakeholder engagement in VET in Portugal
content: The peer review provided an opportunity to reflect on how stakeholders, particularly employers, can contribute more effectively to quality assurance (QA) in vocational education and training (VET).
snippet: The peer review provided an opportunity to reflect on how stakeholders, particularly employers, can contribute more effectively to quality assurance (QA) in vocational education and training (VET).
